title: Setting Up Your Local Sandbox: Running the Mock API Server via Node.js
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: When you're diving into API testing—whether you're using RestAssured, Supertest, Postman, or Karate—the absolute best way to learn is by having your own local sandbox.

title: Dockerizing Your Test Environment: Running the Mock Server via Docker
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In the previous blog, we successfully set up our local mock API server using Node.js. While that works perfectly, configuring Node.js and managing dependencies can sometimes cause headaches.

title: Understanding Core REST: Fetching and Filtering API Data (GET)
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: At the heart of every API test automation framework lies the GET request. In this tutorial, we will explore the core REST endpoints provided by the Mock API Server and extract data using query parameters.

title: Modifying Data: Testing POST, PUT, and DELETE API Endpoints
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Real-world applications don't just read data; they constantly create, update, and delete records. In this post, we will explore the core methods used to modify data in REST architectures: POST, PUT, and DELETE.

title: Mastering Pagination and Sorting in REST API Tests
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In real-world applications, databases hold millions of records. To prevent servers from crashing, APIs return data in small, manageable chunks called pages. In this tutorial, we will learn how to master pagination and sorting.

title: Securing APIs: Testing JWT Authentication Workflows
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In a real-world production environment, you cannot let anyone access sensitive data. Modern APIs use security mechanisms like JSON Web Tokens (JWT) to ensure only authorized users can perform actions.

title: Chaos Engineering: Simulating Latency and Server Timeouts
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In the real world, servers get overloaded and network connections drop. In this tutorial, we will perform Chaos Engineering by intentionally sabotaging our own API requests to simulate severe latency.

title: Beyond REST: An Introduction to GraphQL Querying
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: REST is fantastic, but it has a massive flaw: over-fetching data. Enter GraphQL, a modern query language that allows clients to ask the server for exactly what they need, and nothing more.

title: Advanced GraphQL: Using Variables and Dynamic Filters
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Real applications don't just fetch static data. They fetch dynamic data based on user input. In this tutorial, we will learn the correct way to pass dynamic data into our queries using GraphQL Variables.

title: GraphQL Mutations: Creating and Updating Data
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In REST, we use specific HTTP methods like POST and PUT to modify data. In GraphQL, everything goes through a single endpoint. To modify data, GraphQL introduces the powerful concept of a Mutation.

title: Real-Time Testing: Connecting to WebSockets
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: What if you are testing a live chat application or a stock ticker? To solve this, modern applications use WebSockets. In this tutorial, we will connect to the server's built-in WebSocket chat room.

title: Webhooks: Testing Asynchronous Event Callbacks
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keeping a WebSocket open 24/7 for a single, rare event is a massive waste of server resources. Instead, modern architectures use Webhooks to trigger asynchronous background events and listen for callbacks.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration: Running Tests on Every Commit
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: An automation script is useless if it only runs on your local laptop. In this tutorial, we will pull everything together by integrating our tests directly into a GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline!

title: Capstone Project: Building an End-to-End Test Suite
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: It is now time to prove everything you have learned. In this final Capstone Project, you will build a comprehensive test suite that touches every feature of the mock server in a single execution flow.

title: Testing Real-Time WebSocket Connections in Postman
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Postman isn't just for HTTP requests. It has robust, native support for WebSockets. In this tutorial, we will use Postman to connect to a live WebSocket server.

title: Automating GraphQL Queries and Mutations in Postman
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: GraphQL solves over-fetching by allowing explicit queries. In this tutorial, we will execute GraphQL Queries and Mutations natively within Postman.

title: End-to-End Postman Capstone
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: To prove that you are an API Automation Master, it is time to build a comprehensive, portfolio-grade End-to-End Postman Collection.

title: Introduction to REST APIs & HTTP Methods
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Before we write a single line of Java code, we must understand the language of the web. Modern web applications communicate through REST APIs.

title: Setting up RestAssured with Java
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Now that we understand how REST APIs work, it's time to build our automation framework using Java 17, RestAssured, and TestNG.

title: Writing your First GET/POST Request
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In this tutorial, we will use RestAssured to extract information from our MyCodeYatra Mock API Server using a GET request, and then we will create entirely new data using a POST request.

title: Handling Query Parameters & Path Variables
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Real-world API testing requires dynamic URL construction. In this tutorial, we will use RestAssured to inject Query Parameters and Path Variables dynamically.

title: Advanced JSON Parsing with Jackson
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master API automation by serializing and deserializing JSON payloads dynamically using the powerful Jackson library in RestAssured.

title: API Authentication and Authorization
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to securely automate API endpoints by handling Basic Auth and Bearer Tokens using RestAssured's built-in authentication mechanisms.

title: Parsing and Validating XML Responses
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to effortlessly parse, navigate, and validate XML payloads in legacy or enterprise APIs using RestAssured's built-in xmlPath() tools.

title: Handling File Uploads and Downloads
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Discover how to automate file upload forms using multipart/form-data and successfully download and parse CSVs and binary files with RestAssured.

title: Testing GraphQL APIs with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Demystify GraphQL testing by learning how to automate Queries and Mutations seamlessly using RestAssured's powerful JSON processing capabilities.

title: Generating Allure Reports with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Elevate your API automation framework by integrating Allure Reports with TestNG and RestAssured to automatically capture and display HTTP request and response payloads.

title: Data-Driven API Testing with RestAssured and TestNG
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master Data-Driven Testing (DDT) by integrating TestNG DataProviders into your RestAssured framework to execute massive datasets through a single automated script.

title: Deserializing APIs into POJOs
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to eliminate raw JSON string parsing by automatically mapping HTTP API responses directly into strongly-typed Java objects using POJOs and Lombok.

title: JSON Schema Validation in RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Replace dozens of fragile assertions by learning how to validate entire API payload structures and data types using RestAssured's JSON Schema Validator.

title: Mocking APIs with WireMock and RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Eliminate flaky automated tests by simulating third-party HTTP dependencies locally using WireMock's powerful stubbing and verification capabilities.

title: RestAssured in CI/CD Pipelines
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to integrate your automated RestAssured API test suites into GitHub Actions and Jenkins pipelines to guarantee production stability.

title: Introduction to Observability-Driven Testing
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Move beyond traditional Black-Box API testing. Learn the core concepts of Observability-Driven Testing (ODT) and how asserting distributed traces is revolutionizing QA.

title: OpenTelemetry Integration with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to inject W3C Trace Context headers into your RestAssured tests to initiate distributed OpenTelemetry traces across your backend microservices.

title: Tracetest Deep Dive with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: The grand finale! Master Observability-Driven Testing by writing fluent assertions against your distributed backend databases and message queues using Tracetest.

title: Introduction to SuperTest & Express.js
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Kickstart your NodeJS API Testing journey! Learn what SuperTest is, why it's the industry standard, and write your first asynchronous GET request using Jest and TypeScript.

title: Setting up Supertest with Jest/Vitest
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to build a robust NodeJS API testing framework from scratch using SuperTest, Jest, and TypeScript, including powerful testing hooks.

title: Testing CRUD Operations
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master the core of API testing by writing an End-to-End Test Suite that sequentially validates the entire Create, Read, Update, and Delete lifecycle.

title: TypeScript Interfaces for Payloads
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to transform SuperTest into an enterprise-grade framework by enforcing strict typings on your API Requests using TypeScript Interfaces.

title: Handling Async/Await & Promises
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Prevent flaky tests by mastering NodeJS asynchronous execution. Learn how to handle Promises, Async/Await, and parallel HTTP requests in SuperTest.

title: Authentication & Authorization
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to bypass security gateways in your test automation suite by mastering Basic Auth and dynamic JWT Bearer Tokens in SuperTest.

title: Testing GraphQL APIs
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Demystify GraphQL test automation. Learn how to execute Queries and Mutations by passing schema strings into SuperTest HTTP POST payloads.

title: Testing WebSocket Connections
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to overcome SuperTest's HTTP limitations by integrating the 'ws' library to automate real-time WebSocket communication in Jest.

title: Testing File Uploads & Downloads
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master binary payloads and multipart form boundaries. Learn how to effortlessly test file uploads and downloads using SuperTest.

title: Handling Request Timeouts & Chaos Testing
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Ensure your CI/CD pipeline never hangs again. Master SuperTest timeout controls and learn how to test against unexpected API failures.

title: Integrating Faker.js for Test Data
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Eliminate brittle hardcoded payloads. Learn how to integrate Faker.js with SuperTest to generate massive amounts of dynamic, realistic API test data.

title: Setting up Global Setup & Teardown Hooks
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Architect scalable automation frameworks by mastering Jest lifecycle hooks. Learn how to manage state, databases, and Auth tokens efficiently across SuperTest suites.

title: Mocking Axios / Fetch Requests
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keep your API tests fast, deterministic, and free from third-party rate limits. Learn how to intercept outbound network requests using jest.mock().

title: Generating Test Coverage Reports
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Stop guessing if your APIs are fully tested. Learn how to generate beautiful tabular test coverage reports using Jest and SuperTest to catch missing edge cases.

title: Configuring CI/CD for Jest/SuperTest
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Never merge a broken API again. Learn how to configure GitHub Actions to automatically execute your SuperTest suite on every pull request.

title: Best Practices & Folder Architecture
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Scale your API testing frameworks to thousands of tests. Discover the ultimate enterprise folder architecture and coding standards for SuperTest suites.

title: Introduction to Karate DSL
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Welcome to the Karate API Testing Series! Enter the realm of Behavior Driven Development (BDD) with Karate DSL and write your very first feature file.

title: Setting up Karate and Environment Switching
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to structure your Karate repository for the real world! Configure dynamic environments using karate-config.js and execute folder-specific test runners.

title: JSON and XML Expressions
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Discover the true power of Karate DSL! Learn how to assert complex JSON objects and deeply nested XML responses natively without writing a single Java POJO.

title: Calling other Feature Files
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keep your testing codebase DRY! Learn how to create reusable authentication scripts in Karate and dynamically pass variables between multiple feature files.

title: Data Driven Testing with CSVs
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master Data-Driven Testing in Karate! Learn how to dynamically execute Scenarios using external CSV datasets without writing a single line of parsing code.

title: Reading JSON Payloads from Files
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keep your Karate feature files clean! Learn how to read external JSON files natively, dynamically mutate their contents, and use them as API request payloads.

title: Type Conversion & Data Generators
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Bypass API uniqueness constraints! Learn how to seamlessly invoke Java code directly from your Karate scripts to generate random data, and master native type conversions.

title: Karate UI Testing & API Combinations
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Ditch Selenium! Learn how to combine lightning-fast API calls for test setup with native Chrome UI automation in a single Karate script.

title: Parallel Execution & Generating Cucumber Reports
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Supercharge your test suite! Learn how to run your Karate API tests concurrently across multiple threads and generate stunning visual HTML reports.

title: CI/CD Integration & Mocking with Karate
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master enterprise testing! Learn how to build native backend Mock Servers using Gherkin, and completely automate your test suite in a GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline.

title: Docker Desktop Beginner Guide: Install, Tour the Dashboard, and Run Your First Container
author: Abhishek-sinha
excerpt: A hands-on beginner guide to Docker Desktop on Windows and Mac. Install it, learn the dashboard, run your first container, and pull images from Docker Hub - all in under 30 minutes.

title: Introduction to Event-Driven Testing
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Dive into the paradigm shift from request-response testing to event-driven testing. Understand asynchronous flows, decoupled architectures, and how testing event streams differs from REST APIs.

title: Setting up a Local Kafka Broker
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to easily spin up a multi-container local testing environment containing Apache Kafka and Zookeeper using Docker Compose.

title: Understanding Kafka Architecture
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master the fundamental concepts of topics, partitions, offsets, and consumer groups, and learn why they are critical when designing test automation pipelines.

title: Writing your first Kafka Producer Test
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Step-by-step guide to writing a Kafka producer client and verifying that messages are successfully published to topics.

title: Writing your first Kafka Consumer Test
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to set up a subscription poll loop, fetch messages, and assert their key and value payloads in automated tests.

title: Schema Registry Validation
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Ensure message integrity! Learn how to test event formats (Avro, Protobuf, JSON) against a Confluent Schema Registry.

title: Validating Message Headers
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Test your metadata. Learn how to verify correlation IDs, partition keys, and tracing metadata stored in Kafka headers.

title: Handling Eventual Consistency
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Say goodbye to Thread.sleep()! Master wait strategies and async assertions using Awaitility for asynchronous testing.

title: Testing Kafka Streams
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to validate stream processing topologies, joins, map operations, and windowed aggregations without a live broker.

title: Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Testing
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Ensure reliability! Validate error-handling mechanisms and ensure corrupt payloads are routed to retry and DLQ topics.

title: Building a Kafka Test Utility
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Simplify your automation codebase. Build a reusable Kafka producer and consumer helper to encapsulate complex boilerplates.

title: Testcontainers for Kafka
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Say goodbye to pre-configured brokers! Learn how to spin up ephemeral, lightweight Kafka containers on-the-fly inside JUnit.

title: Mocking Kafka
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Speed up unit testing! Learn how to use MockProducer and MockConsumer from kafka-clients for super-fast offline validation.

title: Load Testing Kafka
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Test scale! Build high-performance load tests to push millions of events per second into Kafka brokers to measure consumer lag.

title: Integrating Kafka Tests in CI/CD
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Complete the cycle! Learn how to run Zookeeper, Kafka, and your test suites automatically in a GitHub Actions workflow.

title: What is Selenium with Java?
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Welcome to the Selenium with Java Series! In this guide, we dive into the foundations of Selenium WebDriver and write your first automation script targeting a live site.

title: Selenium vs Playwright vs Cypress: The Ultimate Showdown
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Selecting the right web UI testing framework is a critical engineering decision. We compare Selenium, Playwright, and Cypress across architecture, language support, and features.

title: Setup Selenium with Java Project: The Complete Modern Guide
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Setting up an enterprise-grade automation framework requires a clean, repeatable project structure. We set up a project using Maven and the native Selenium Manager.

title: Selenium WebDriver Architecture & W3C Protocol
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Understanding the underlying communication protocol is essential to building stable frameworks. We dive into how language bindings, browser drivers, and browsers interact under the W3C WebDriver standard.

title: Mastering Selenium WebDriver Lifecycle: Launch, Quit & Resource Management
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Improper resource management is the #1 cause of flaky tests and crashed CI/CD runner nodes. Learn how to manage the lifecycle of your Selenium WebDriver instances, prevent memory leaks, and cleanly dispose of driver sessions.

title: Locators in Selenium: The Complete Guide to Locating Elements
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Locators are the foundation of any web automation framework. Learn how to write stable, robust locators using ID, Name, CSS Selectors, and XPath while avoiding common design pitfalls.

title: Assertions in Test Automation: Hard, Soft, and Fluent Assertions
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: An automated test without assertions is not a test. Learn the differences between Hard, Soft, and Fluent AssertJ assertions, when to use each style, and common design pitfalls.

title: Writing Your First E2E Test: A Step-by-Step Guide with Selenium and TestNG
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to write and execute your first End-to-End (E2E) automated login test using Selenium WebDriver, Java, and TestNG, complete with invalid and valid test scenario validations.

title: Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) and Browser Logging in Selenium
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to leverage the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) and collect browser console logs using Selenium WebDriver in Java, featuring a complete step-by-step TestNG automation guide.

title: Capturing Screenshots and Failure Evidence in Selenium
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to collect screenshots and automated failure evidence in your test suites using Selenium WebDriver in Java, featuring a complete step-by-step TestNG automation guide.

title: Understanding Wait Strategies in Selenium: Implicit, Explicit, and Fluent Waits
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master synchronization in Selenium WebDriver. Learn the structural and runtime differences between Implicit, Explicit, and Fluent Waits, and how to automate dynamic elements in Java.

title: Forms Handling in Selenium: Automating Inputs, Dropdowns, and Checkboxes
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: A complete guide to automating forms handling in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to interact with inputs, radio buttons, checkboxes, single-select and multi-select dropdowns in Java.

title: Handling JavaScript Alerts, Confirm Dialogs, and Prompts in Selenium
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: A complete guide to handling browser native dialogs in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to switch to, accept, dismiss, and input text into alerts, confirms, and prompts in Java.

title: Automating File Uploads in Selenium: SendKeys, Robot, and AutoIt Strategies
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master file upload automation in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to upload files using the standard sendKeys strategy, Java Robot class, and AutoIt scripts in Java.

title: Automating File Downloads in Selenium: Preferences and Verification
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: A complete guide to automating file downloads in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to configure browser preferences to download files to custom folders headlessly in Java.

title: Automating Windows and Tabs in Selenium WebDriver: Handles & Context Switching
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: A comprehensive tutorial on managing multiple windows and browser tabs in Selenium using Java. Master window handles, switching techniques, and Selenium 4's newWindow API.

title: Automating iFrames & Frames in Selenium WebDriver: Switching and Context Control
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master context switching for Inline Frames (iFrames) and Frames in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to switch contexts, interact with nested elements, and return to default content in Java.

title: Automating Shadow DOM in Selenium WebDriver: Native getShadowRoot and JS Strategies
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: A complete guide to automating Shadow DOM elements in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to use Selenium 4's native getShadowRoot API and JavaScript Executor to bypass DOM boundaries in Java.

title: Automating Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) in Selenium: Network and Console Control
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master native integration with Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) in Selenium 4. Learn how to perform network throttling, location emulation, and console log capture using version-independent commands in Java.

title: Automating Parallel Execution in Selenium WebDriver: Thread-Safety & TestNG
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master parallel test execution in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to configure thread-safe driver sessions using the ThreadLocal pattern and trigger concurrent execution in TestNG.

title: Automating Cross-Browser Testing in Selenium: Chrome, Firefox, and Edge Strategies
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master cross-browser test automation in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to configure dynamic, parameter-driven test execution across Chrome, Firefox, and Edge engines in Java.

title: Automating Data-Driven Testing in Selenium: TestNG DataProviders & Apache POI
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master data-driven testing in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to parse Excel files using Apache POI and feed inputs dynamically to TestNG DataProviders in Java.

title: Automating Page Object Model (POM) in Selenium: Design & Encapsulation
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master the Page Object Model (POM) design pattern in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to encapsulate locator elements, build reusable page methods, and cleanly write assertions in Java.

title: Driver Factory Pattern in Selenium: Designing a Thread-Safe Singleton
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master the Driver Factory Pattern in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to implement a thread-safe singleton DriverFactory using ThreadLocal, configure cross-browser headless runs, and ensure safe lifecycle cleanup.

title: Builder Pattern in Selenium: Simplifying Dynamic Test Data Generation
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master the Builder Pattern in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to construct clean, immutable test data payloads using the Builder design pattern in Java.

title: Screenplay Pattern in Selenium: Refactoring POM with SOLID Principles
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master the Screenplay Pattern in Selenium WebDriver. Learn how to refactor bloated Page Objects (POM) into clean, decoupled Actors, Abilities, Tasks, and Questions in Java.

title: TestNG Lifecycle & Listeners in Selenium: Mastering Test Hooks and Event Interception
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master TestNG lifecycle annotations (@Before/@After) and build a custom ITestListener and IRetryAnalyzer in Java Selenium to intercept every test event and handle flaky tests automatically.

title: Utility Framework in Selenium Java: Building Reusable FileUtil, JsonUtil and StringUtil
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Build a professional Selenium utility layer in Java with FileUtil, JsonUtil, and StringUtil. Learn how to eliminate boilerplate file, JSON, and string operations from test classes using clean, reusable helpers.

title: Configuration Management in Selenium Java: Dynamic Properties and Environment-Specific Config
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Eliminate hard-coded values from Selenium tests with a thread-safe ConfigManager in Java. Load base defaults and environment-specific overlays dynamically using system properties and .properties files.

title: Logging Framework in Selenium Java: Integrating Log4j2 for Structured Test Logs
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Replace System.out.println with professional Log4j2 logging in your Selenium Java framework. Configure console and rolling file appenders, set per-package log levels, and use parameterised messages for zero-overhead logging.

title: Framework Architecture: Structuring an Enterprise Selenium Java Project
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Design an enterprise-grade folder structure for a Selenium Java framework. Leverage the standard Maven directory layout and enforce strict Separation of Concerns (SoC) for scalable test automation.

title: Spring DI & Testcontainers: Modernizing Selenium Java Architecture
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Replace rigid static factories with Spring Dependency Injection (DI) and fragile grids with Testcontainers. Learn how to spin up ephemeral Docker environments on the fly for scalable UI automation.

title: Custom TestNG Listeners: Advanced Reporting and Retry Logic
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Build custom TestNG listeners to automate screenshot capture on failure, log errors cleanly, and retry flaky tests using IRetryAnalyzer and IAnnotationTransformer.

title: Parallel Execution with ThreadLocal: Scaling Your Selenium Java Framework
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Achieve robust and scalable parallel execution in your Selenium Java framework by eliminating static factories and leveraging Java's ThreadLocal alongside TestNG.
